questions.question: How many carbon chiral centers does oxytocin have?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Oxytocin contains more than 9 carbon chiral centers.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Oxytocin contains more than 10 carbon chiral centers.
explanations.c: Correct. Oxytocin contains 11 carbon chiral centers derived from its amino acid residues.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Oxytocin contains significantly more than 8 carbon chiral centers.

questions.question: Oxytocin is a peptide of ________ amino acids.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Oxytocin contains more than five amino acids.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Oxytocin contains more than six amino acids.
explanations.c: Correct. Oxytocin is a nonapeptide composed of nine amino acids.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Oxytocin contains more than four amino acids.

explanations.a: Incorrect. This is not the molecular formula of oxytocin.
explanations.b: Correct. The molecular formula of oxytocin is C43H66N12O12S2.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Oxytocin does not contain three sulfur atoms.
explanations.d: Incorrect. This formula does not match oxytocin.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Adenohypophysis refers to the anterior pituitary gland.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Zona glomerulosa is a part of the adrenal cortex.
explanations.c: Correct. Oxytocin is released from the neurohypophysis (posterior pituitary).
explanations.d: Incorrect. Pars intermedia is a different region of the pituitary gland.

questions.question: Which two hormones are released from the posterior lobe of the pituitary gland?

explanations.a: Incorrect. ACTH and TSH are secreted by the anterior pituitary.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Growth hormone (GH) is secreted by the anterior pituitary.
explanations.c: Correct. The posterior pituitary releases ADH (vasopressin) and oxytocin.
explanations.d: Incorrect. TRH and CRH are hypothalamic releasing hormones.
